channel.title: Safety on Research Portfolio
channel.description: Recent content in Safety on Research Portfolio

item.title: LoRA Reward Model for Sparse-Reward, Human-Aligned SoC Training
item.description:
Problem

Placeholder: describe the challenge of LLM-as-a-judge variance and sparse-reward SoC training.

Why the problem is hard

Placeholder: why human-aligned reward learning and SoC under sparse rewards are difficult.

System design

Placeholder: high-level pipeline (curated datasets → LoRA reward model → SoC training).

Architecture diagram

Implementation details

Placeholder: dataset curation, LoRA setup, reward annotation protocol, training details.

Experiments

Placeholder: baselines, metrics (e.g. judge variance, SoC success rate), ablations.

item.title: Multi-Turn Jailbreak Datasets and Post-Training Hardening (SFT + DPO)
item.description:
Problem

Placeholder: the need for robust multi-turn jailbreak defenses and compliant refusal/redirection.

Why the problem is hard

Placeholder: why automated pipeline design and post-training alignment for jailbreaks are challenging.

System design

Placeholder: SoC attack pipeline → dataset transformation → SFT + DPO workflow.

Architecture diagram

Implementation details

Placeholder: attack construction, (query, refusal/redirection) data format, SFT/DPO setup.

Experiments

Placeholder: baselines, multi-turn jailbreak benchmarks, refusal/redirection quality metrics.

Results

Placeholder: tables or figures on attack success rate vs. defense, compliance, and usability.

item.title: PEFT Defense Agent and Real-Time Output Gating Under Adversarial Retrieval
item.description:
Problem

Placeholder: adversarial retrieval corruption and need for real-time gating without losing task utility.

Why the problem is hard

Placeholder: why discriminating safe vs. unsafe states from (query, context) and gating in real time are challenging.

System design

Placeholder: PEFT SFT on (query, context) → classifier → output gating layer.

Architecture diagram

Implementation details

Placeholder: PEFT setup, training data, gating logic, corruption scenarios.

Experiments

Placeholder: attack success with/without gating, task accuracy under corruption, latency.

item.title: Safety Reasoning Models for Interpretability and Jailbreak Diagnostics
item.description:
Problem

Placeholder: need for interpretable safety diagnostics and reasoning over jailbreak points.

Why the problem is hard

Placeholder: why teaching explicit reasoning and mining preference pairs from reasoning traces are difficult.

System design

Placeholder: SFT for reasoning + intent → preference mining from perturbations and bad traces → DPO.

Architecture diagram

Implementation details

Placeholder: reasoning format, intent labels, perturbation strategy, DPO data pipeline.

Experiments

Placeholder: diagnostic accuracy, reasoning quality, alignment metrics.
